seo.title: Building Data Extraction with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: BeeAI enables robust data extraction from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls, powering production AI agents with memory and tools. Vex integration provides essential guardrails to address common failure modes like unbounded memory growth and tool injection risks. This guide ensures reliable, verifiable extractions critical for medium-risk production workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
BeeAI agents process unstructured documents through a schema-guided extraction pipeline. The agent loads documents, invokes Granite models with Pydantic schemas for structured output, and persists state across sessions using BeeAI's memory layer. Tools validate extracted fields against business rules before storage or downstream processing.

Key components include the BeeAI Agent with memory for session continuity, schema-defined extraction tools, and event hooks for observability. Long-running extractions risk memory bloat from accumulating document states, while schema inconsistencies from Granite's variable formatting can lead to incomplete or invalid outputs.

Vex listens to BeeAI events to enforce schema compliance, field accuracy, and completeness, preventing propagation of extraction errors.

risks.name: Unbounded Agent Memory Growth
risks.description: Long extraction sessions accumulate document states in BeeAI memory, causing OOM errors
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory usage per session and triggers truncation at thresholds

risks.name: Tool Injection via Malformed Documents
risks.description: Adversarial PDFs trigger unsafe tool calls through validation gaps
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and tool call whitelisting before execution

risks.name: Granite Output Format Drift
risks.description: Model variations break Pydantic schema parsing during extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex schema compliance checks with fallback parsing strategies

risks.name: Incomplete Field Extraction
risks.description: LLM misses required fields despite schema guidance
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness validation with field population rate monitoring

implementation.title: Schema-Defined Extraction Agent
implementation.description: Define Pydantic schema and BeeAI agent for document extraction

implementation.title: Document Processing Pipeline
implementation.description: Load and preprocess documents for extraction
implementation.code:
import PyPDF2

def load_documents(pdf_path: str) -> list[str]:
    texts = []
    with open(pdf_path, 'rb') as file:
        reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(file)
        for page in reader.pages:
            texts.append(page.extract_text())
    return texts

agent = ExtractionAgent()
documents = load_documents('invoices.pdf')
results = []
for doc in documents:
    result = agent.extract_invoice(doc)
    results.append(result.model_dump())
